prompt: What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for the human TP53 protein? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for the human BRCA1 protein? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the length, in residues, of the canonical isoform of the human BRCA1 UniProt entry (P38398)? Reply with the integer alone.
What is the gene name (HGNC symbol) recorded on UniProt entry P04637? Reply with the symbol alone.
What organism does UniProt entry P0DTC2 belong to? Reply with the organism's scientific name alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for human insulin? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for the human hemoglobin subunit beta (HBB) protein? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for human KRAS? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for human EGFR? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for human dystrophin (DMD)? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the canonical UniProt keyword identifier (KW-NNNN) for the post-translational modification 'Acetylation'? Reply with the KW-NNNN identifier alone.
What is the canonical UniProt keyword identifier (KW-NNNN) for the post-translational modification 'Glycoprotein'? Reply with the KW-NNNN identifier alone.
What is the canonical UniProt subcellular-location identifier (SL-NNNN) for 'Cell membrane'? Reply with the SL-NNNN identifier alone.
What is the canonical UniProt subcellular-location identifier (SL-NNNN) for 'Nucleus'? Reply with the SL-NNNN identifier alone.
What is the canonical UniProt subcellular-location identifier (SL-NNNN) for 'Mitochondrion'? Reply with the SL-NNNN identifier alone.
What are the first 10 amino-acid residues of the canonical FASTA sequence of UniProt P04637 (human TP53)? Reply with the 10-letter single-letter amino-acid string alone, all uppercase, no separators.
What is the UniRef cluster identifier at 100% sequence identity whose representative member is human TP53 canonical (UniProt P04637)? Reply with the UniRef ID alone.
What is the UniRef cluster identifier at 50% sequence identity whose representative member is human TP53 canonical (UniProt P04637)? Reply with the UniRef ID alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for the human TP53 paralogue TP63? Reply with the accession alone.
What is the canonical UniProt Swiss-Prot accession for the human TP53 paralogue TP73? Reply with the accession alone.

Map the following human gene symbols to canonical reviewed UniProt accessions: TP53, BRCA1, BRCA2, EGFR, KRAS. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are the gene symbols and values are the accessions, e.g. {"TP53": "P04637", ...}.

Map the following subcellular-component names to their canonical UniProt SL-NNNN identifiers: Cell membrane, Cytoplasm, Nucleus, Mitochondrion, Endoplasmic reticulum. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are the names and values are the SL-NNNN identifiers.

Map the following PTM names to their canonical UniProt KW-NNNN keyword identifiers: Acetylation, Phosphoprotein, Glycoprotein, Methylation, Disulfide bond. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are the names and values are the KW-NNNN identifiers.

For human TP53 canonical (UniProt P04637), list the cluster identifiers across the three UniRef identity tiers: 50%, 90%, 100%. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are '50', '90', '100' and values are the UniRef cluster IDs.
Map the human RAS-family oncogene symbols to canonical reviewed UniProt accessions: KRAS, NRAS, HRAS. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are the gene symbols and values are the accessions.
Map the human TP53 paralogue family to canonical reviewed UniProt accessions: TP53, TP63, TP73. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are the gene symbols and values are the accessions.

Map the following human cytochrome-P450 enzyme symbols to canonical reviewed UniProt accessions: CYP3A4, CYP2D6, CYP1A2. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are the gene symbols and values are the accessions.

For UniProt P04637 (human TP53), list every distinct feature 'type' value present in the canonical entry. Reply with a JSON array of unique type strings, alphabetically sorted, e.g. ["Active site", "Chain", ...].

For UniProt P04637 (human TP53), list every database name appearing in the entry's uniProtKBCrossReferences for which there is at least one cross-reference. Reply with a JSON array of unique database name strings, alphabetically sorted.
Map the human Hox-A cluster gene symbols HOXA1, HOXA9, HOXA13 to canonical reviewed UniProt accessions. Reply with a JSON object whose keys are the gene symbols and values are the accessions.
